steps.content: Apply the rule $x^{a}\cdot x^{b}=x^{a+b}$ to each product. First term: $x^{-6+4}y^{3}z^{5+5}=x^{-2}y^{3}z^{10}$. Second term: $x^{-6}y^{3+8}z^{5+(-7)}=x^{-6}y^{11}z^{-2}$.

Combine to get $x^{-2}y^{3}z^{10}+x^{-6}y^{11}z^{-2}$. Evaluate each choice:
  • "${x}^{-2}{z}^{10}+{y}^{11}{z}^{-2}$": missing the $y^{3}$ factor in the first term and the $x^{-6}$ factor in the second term. Incorrect.
  • "${x}^{-2}{z}^{10}+{x}^{-6}{z}^{-2}$": missing $y^{3}$ in the first term and $y^{11}$ in the second term. Incorrect.
  • "${x}^{-2}{y}^{3}{z}^{10}+{y}^{8}{z}^{-7}$": the first term is correct but the second term retains the original $y^{8}z^{-7}$ without the $x^{-6}y^{3}$ factors applied. Incorrect.
  • "${x}^{-2}{y}^{3}{z}^{10}+{x}^{-6}{y}^{11}{z}^{-2}$": matches both computed terms exactly. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
